overview: Refactor the existing LiveChat-Bot codebase for professional maintainability, modularity, security, and correctness while preserving all observable behavior and backward compatibility across REST, Socket.IO, Discord commands, environment contracts, and database behavior.
goals: Restructure the entire codebase into clear, cohesive modules with stable boundaries and minimal coupling.
Improve readability and maintainability through consistent naming, file organization, and conventions.
Strengthen security posture with stricter input validation, safer error handling, and secure runtime defaults.
Increase correctness with explicit TypeScript typing and stricter compiler/linting guardrails.
Keep full backward compatibility with no breaking behavior changes.
Use characterization coverage to lock current behavior before and during refactor slices.
Apply patch-level dependency hygiene to reduce risk while avoiding disruptive upgrades.
nonGoals: No feature additions or product behavior redesign.
No changes to public API contracts, Socket event contracts, Discord command UX, or environment variable names/semantics.
No schema-breaking data model migrations or edits to prisma/migrations files.
successMetrics: All listed external compatibility surfaces produce no contract diffs against characterization baselines.
`pnpm lint` passes at story completion gates.
`pnpm build` passes at story completion gates.
Every touched module has updated characterization coverage with at least one positive and one negative scenario.
Implicit any usage is eliminated in touched code paths and tracked exceptions are documented with owners and removal plan.
Patch dependency updates complete without behavior regressions in characterization checks.
openQuestions: Confirm canonical list of externally consumed routes/events beyond currently known overlay/admin/ingest paths.
Confirm whether any private operational scripts rely on current internal file paths that need temporary compatibility re-exports.
Decide timeline for removing temporary TypeScript strictness exceptions after initial refactor merge.

stack.auth: Existing Discord token auth, overlay client token flow, and ingest pairing/token flow preserved

routes.name: Admin UI
routes.purpose: Serve admin interface without changing existing behavior or markup semantics

routes.name: Admin Overview API
routes.purpose: Expose runtime/admin overview data with unchanged payload shape

routes.name: Admin Runtime Settings API
routes.purpose: Read runtime settings with unchanged response and validation behavior

routes.name: Admin Ingest Authors API
routes.purpose: List known ingest authors with unchanged filtering/sorting semantics

routes.name: Overlay Client Revoke API
routes.purpose: Revoke overlay clients with unchanged authorization and response behavior

routes.name: Overlay Pair Consume
routes.purpose: Consume pairing codes for overlay clients with unchanged request/response contract

routes.name: Overlay Config
routes.purpose: Serve overlay runtime configuration with unchanged payload contract

routes.name: Overlay Media Access
routes.purpose: Serve media assets with unchanged authorization/streaming behavior

routes.name: Transient Media Compatibility Endpoint
routes.purpose: Preserve backward-compatible transient media contract where deployed integrations depend on it

routes.name: Ingest Pair Consume
routes.purpose: Consume ingest pairing codes with unchanged request/response contract

uiNotes: No frontend redesign: admin interface behavior, layout structure, and field semantics remain unchanged.
Discord command outputs (message content, embeds, and permission outcomes) remain unchanged.
If internal templates are reorganized, route outputs and client-visible text remain byte-for-byte compatible where feasible.

importFormat.description: No new import format is introduced; existing API payload contracts and command inputs are preserved. Characterization fixtures capture baseline request/response shapes to enforce compatibility.

rules: Behavior preservation is mandatory: external outputs and side effects must match existing behavior unless explicitly approved.
Refactor in thin slices with characterization coverage added before structural change in each area.
No breaking changes to REST overlay endpoints, socket events from @livechat/overlay-protocol, Discord command interfaces, env variable contracts, or Prisma model semantics.
No edits to prisma/migrations/** during this refactor.
Use centralized validation/parsing utilities to avoid duplicated unsafe coercion logic.
All new/updated error paths must return safe, non-sensitive responses while preserving status codes/body semantics.
TypeScript strictness is tightened with explicit temporary exceptions only when unavoidable; each exception must be tracked with owner and removal task.
Patch-only dependency upgrades are allowed; minor/major upgrades are out of scope.
Quality gates run after each meaningful slice: pnpm lint and pnpm build.
If any compatibility diff appears, stop and resolve before proceeding to the next story.

stories.title: Establish characterization baselines for current behavior
stories.description: As a maintainer, I want executable characterization coverage for current contracts so that refactoring can proceed without accidental behavior drift.
acceptanceCriteria: Create characterization scripts/tests for REST, Socket.IO lifecycle, Discord command execution flow, and media lifecycle critical paths in touched modules.
Baseline artifacts capture request/response status codes, payload shapes, and key side effects for sensitive routes and events.
Example: valid POST /overlay/pair/consume returns the same status code and response shape in baseline and post-change runs.
Negative case: malformed consume payload reproduces the same validation error code/body shape as baseline.
A runbook documents how to execute characterization checks before each refactor slice.

stories.title: Define module boundaries and compatibility map
stories.description: As a maintainer, I want a target module map with compatibility constraints so that code reorganization remains safe and consistent.
acceptanceCriteria: Create a documented module map covering loaders, components, services, repositories, and shared utilities for the full codebase.
For each moved/renamed internal file, provide compatibility re-export wrappers where internal consumers need a phased transition.
Example: existing internal import path continues to resolve via compatibility wrapper during migration phase.
Negative case: any proposed module move that would alter an external entrypoint is rejected and logged as out of scope.
Boundary rules include prohibited cross-layer dependencies and approved dependency directions.

stories.title: Refactor REST route loading into domain modules
stories.description: As a maintainer, I want REST route registration and handlers organized by domain so that HTTP behavior is easier to maintain without changing contracts.
acceptanceCriteria: Reorganize REST loader internals into clearly separated domain registrars while preserving route prefixes, methods, and handler behavior.
Maintain existing response payload keys and status codes for admin, overlay, and ingest routes.
Example: GET /overlay/config returns the same fields and status code before and after refactor.
Negative case: unsupported route paths continue to return the same not-found behavior as baseline.
Characterization checks for REST routes pass with no contract diff.

stories.title: Refactor socket loader lifecycle with strict boundaries
stories.description: As a maintainer, I want socket authentication, session lifecycle, and event emission separated into explicit modules so that correctness and security are easier to enforce.
acceptanceCriteria: Split socket loader internals into authentication, connection state, and event dispatch modules with explicit typed interfaces.
Preserve event names and payload contract from @livechat/overlay-protocol.
Example: authenticated overlay client receives the same event sequence and payload structure as baseline.
Negative case: invalid token or malformed payload follows the same rejection/disconnect semantics as baseline.
No new global mutable state is introduced outside existing runtime wiring constraints.

stories.title: Refactor Discord command loading and execution flow
stories.description: As a maintainer, I want Discord command registration and execution internals modularized so that command maintenance is safer and clearer without user-visible changes.
acceptanceCriteria: Split Discord loader responsibilities into command registry, command metadata assembly, and interaction execution handling.
Keep command names, options, permission expectations, and user-facing responses unchanged.
Example: /help command returns the same embed/message content and status behavior as baseline.
Negative case: unknown or failing command interaction is handled safely without process crash and with baseline-equivalent user-facing outcome.
Characterization checks confirm no contract drift in command registration payload shape.

stories.title: Centralize input validation and request parsing
stories.description: As a maintainer, I want shared validation and parsing utilities so that inputs are consistently sanitized and typed across domains.
acceptanceCriteria: Introduce shared validation helpers (using existing validation approach) for common request body/params/query patterns.
Apply shared validators to sensitive REST and socket input paths without changing success/error contract semantics.
Example: valid admin payload (including expected author identifiers) is accepted and persisted exactly as before.
Negative case: invalid authorDiscordUserId still yields baseline-compatible invalid_author_discord_user_id behavior.
All new validators expose explicit TypeScript input/output types.

stories.title: Standardize safe error handling and logging
stories.description: As an operator, I want consistent error handling and safe logs so that failures are diagnosable without leaking sensitive details.
acceptanceCriteria: Create typed error categories and centralized mapping to HTTP/socket/command error outputs while preserving current observable status/body behavior.
Ensure sensitive values (tokens, secrets, raw credentials) are never logged.
Example: known operational error logs structured context and returns the same user-visible error response contract.
Negative case: unexpected thrown error does not crash long-running services and still returns baseline-equivalent safe response.
Error-handling paths are covered by characterization scenarios in touched modules.

stories.title: Harden environment parsing and secure defaults
stories.description: As a maintainer, I want stricter runtime config parsing and secure defaults so that invalid states are reduced without altering valid deployment behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Refactor env parsing into clearly typed config modules with explicit defaults and invariant checks.
Preserve behavior for all currently valid environment configurations.
Example: existing production-equivalent env values produce identical runtime configuration outputs.
Negative case: clearly invalid config value is rejected deterministically with safe logging and controlled startup failure behavior.
No environment variable is renamed or removed; backward-compatible aliases are documented if needed.

stories.title: Tighten TypeScript compiler policy with tracked exceptions
stories.description: As a maintainer, I want stricter TypeScript settings and explicit exception tracking so that runtime bugs are prevented earlier.
acceptanceCriteria: Adopt stricter tsconfig policy for refactor target (including noImplicitAny and additional strictness flags) using a phased, non-breaking rollout.
Where full strictness cannot be immediate, add temporary localized exceptions with TODO owner and removal ticket reference.
Example: touched modules compile without implicit any and with explicit interface/type contracts.
Negative case: introducing a new implicit any in a touched path fails build/lint checks.
Document final strictness matrix and remaining exception backlog.

stories.title: Eliminate weak typing in critical runtime paths
stories.description: As a maintainer, I want explicit types/interfaces on critical flows so that domain boundaries and contracts are compile-time enforced.
acceptanceCriteria: Define explicit request/response/domain interfaces for critical loaders, auth flows, and media orchestration paths.
Replace implicit any and unsafe casts in touched critical files with typed narrowing and guards.
Example: overlay auth resolution returns a discriminated union type consumed safely by route and socket handlers.
Negative case: missing required contract field (for example createdByDiscordUserId where required) is caught at compile time or validated at runtime with baseline-compatible behavior.
Add focused type-level tests or compile checks for newly introduced domain types where feasible.

stories.title: Perform dependency hygiene with patch-only updates
stories.description: As a maintainer, I want safe dependency hygiene so that known risks are reduced without introducing compatibility breaks.
acceptanceCriteria: Audit direct dependencies for unused packages, duplicated transitive risks, and available patch updates.
Apply only patch-level upgrades with release note review and rollback notes.
Example: a patch security update is applied and characterization/lint/build checks remain green with no contract changes.
Negative case: minor or major update candidates are explicitly deferred and documented as out of current scope.
Update dependency hygiene report with before/after versions and rationale.

stories.title: Refactor media lifecycle internals for correctness and cleanup safety
stories.description: As an operator, I want media ingestion and cleanup internals simplified and guarded so that temporary files and asset states remain correct under failures.
acceptanceCriteria: Split large media lifecycle logic into focused modules/functions with explicit typed boundaries.
Preserve existing storage paths, media URL behavior, and expiration semantics.
Example: successful media ingest produces the same playable asset behavior and metadata state transitions as baseline.
Negative case: failed download/transcode path cleans temporary resources and does not leave inconsistent asset status beyond baseline behavior.
Characterization checks cover success, timeout/error, and cleanup scenarios.

stories.title: Align naming and formatting conventions across modules
stories.description: As a maintainer, I want consistent naming and code style conventions so that the refactored codebase is easier to navigate and review.
acceptanceCriteria: Apply a documented naming convention for files, symbols, and module responsibilities across all touched paths.
Ensure lint/format outputs are clean and predictable while preserving behavior.
Example: similarly scoped services follow the same naming structure and export style conventions.
Negative case: ambiguous or duplicated naming that obscures behavior is rejected during review and corrected before merge.
Update refactor guide notes for future contributors with examples of accepted conventions.

stories.title: Execute full backward-compatibility verification and release checklist
stories.description: As a maintainer, I want a final verification gate so that the refactor can ship confidently without behavior regressions.
acceptanceCriteria: Run full characterization suite plus required quality gates and capture artifacts for review.
Verify no contract diffs for REST routes, socket events, Discord command behavior, env semantics, and database behavior.
Example: sampled production-like workflows (overlay pairing, ingest pairing, command-driven playback) match baseline outcomes.
Negative case: any detected contract drift blocks completion until explicitly resolved or approved.
Publish a release checklist and residual-risk note that lists anything intentionally not validated.
